# ACMT Partners on AI-Driven Pain Management Education for Military and Veterans

### ACMT Partners on AI-Driven Pain Management Education for Military and Veterans

ACMT is excited to announce its collaboration with [**AKH Inc.**](https://www.akhcme.com/) (Advancing Knowledge in Healthcare) and the Veterans Health and Wellness Foundation (VHWF) on a groundbreaking educational initiative. This AI-driven, adaptive learning program, funded by an Opioid Analgesic REMS grant, focuses on educating healthcare providers about pain management, particularly for military personnel and veterans.

The program, compliant with FDA’s 2023 Blueprint and REMS guidelines, covers critical topics such as optimal opioid prescribing practices, addressing substance use disorders, and balancing the benefits and risks of pain therapy. Designed for a wide range of healthcare professionals, the training emphasizes individualized care for active-duty military and veterans, ensuring safe and effective pain management.

Thank you to Drs. Leslie Dye, Ashley Haynes, and Robert "Cole" Pueringer for being key contributors, providing expertise in substance use disorder management and patient care strategies.

# AI Training for Veterans

A guide to GI Bill & VA Approved AI classes and certificate programs

## Contents

Read more in [AI](/learn/ai)

Share

Are you a veteran returning to civilian life or a member of a veteranâs family aiming to take advantage of entitlements and benefits? This article is designed to help you navigate your options for taking advantage of these services to enter into a professional skills training program and learn the artificial intelligence skills youâve always wanted to learn. A veteran may want to learn artificial intelligence to improve their problem-solving and decision-making capabilities for a variety of career opportunities. Artificial intelligence can open doors to careers in technology, data, manufacturing, and other fields, many of which are well-paying and in high demand. This is an ideal skill for veterans looking to transition from military to civilian life.

These Noble Desktop bootcamps and certificate programs are approved for training veterans and other eligible individuals using the Post-9/11 GI BillÂ® (Chapter 33), the Veteran Readiness and Employment Program (Chapter 31), and the Survivorsâ and Dependentsâ Educational Assistance (Chapter 35).

**Noble Desktop Coding and Web Development Training Courses Approved by the VA:**

## **Support for Veterans Looking to Learn AI**

Support options for veterans such as the GI Bill, Forever GI Bill, and VET TEC program all provide essential aid to help veterans shift from working in the military to an everyday civilian career. The GI Bill is a financial program that offers assistance for education and professional training. It covers tuition and other education-related costs. The Forever GI Bill is an addition to the GI Bill that protects veteranâs benefits and increases the length of time they can access them. The VET TEC program, on the other hand, specifically focuses on technology-based training so veterans can upskill to highly sought-after positions.

![AI Classes: Live & Hands-on, In NYC or Online, Learn From Experts, Free Retake, Small Class Sizes, 1-on-1 Bonus Training. Named a Top Bootcamp by Forbes, Fortune, & Time Out. Noble Desktop. Learn More.]()

### AI Classes & Bootcamps

Named a Top Bootcamp by Forbes, Fortune & Time Out

Although Noble Desktopâs programs do not qualify for funding under the VET TEC program, there are still options for veterans to learn technical skills. In particular, Noble Desktop offers relatively affordable courses, bootcamps, and certificate classes that offer additional benefits and payment plans. Noble Desktop offers VA-supported programs for full-time, in-person participants, so if you are interested, you can explore your options through Noble Desktop on the Veterans Support page.

## **Why Should Veterans Learn Artificial Intelligence?**

Veterans should consider learning artificial intelligence for several reasons. First, it equips them with highly technical skills that are needed across a broad spectrum of job positions and industries. This ensures they will be able to easily find a career path once theyâve left the military. Artificial intelligence includes studying a wide variety of subjects and tools too such as machine learning, data analysis, and automation. With the right training, veterans can easily transition from one career to the next due to their highly desirable skills and the wide variety of jobs available that suit their talents.

Because the artificial intelligence field is so broad, veterans have a lot of options. They can work on improving business decisions and practices or help develop useful tools for consumers such as automated customer service bots. There are also diverse research opportunities to explore and numerous sectors that they can enter. Learning artificial intelligence can serve as a worthwhile investment in the future and help veterans take the next step in their professional lives.

Furthermore, veterans are typically hard-working problem solvers that thrive in settings that require analytical and critical thinking. Their work ethic ensures that tasks get completed correctly and with the help of artificial intelligence, their workflow can be optimal, easy, and efficient. Thorough training in artificial intelligence ensures that veterans can apply their skills in a variety of contexts, not just limited to their military experience. By learning artificial intelligence, veterans can bridge the gap between their military career and a new, invigorating tech-driven career that allows them to have an easy transition into a civilian career.

## **What Can Veterans Do with Artificial Intelligence Skills?**

With proper [artificial intelligence training](/topics/ai-classes), you can explore numerous fields, pursue different career paths, and improve your everyday life. Professionally, artificial intelligence enables businesses to make better decisions and solve both simplistic and complex problems. It is used in various fields including finance, healthcare, retail, and education. You can use artificial intelligence to detect anomalies in financial statements, design customized treatment plans for those suffering from illnesses, and optimize manufacturing processes. Beyond that, artificial intelligence can also help businesses stay in contact with customers with automated customer service and highly skilled chatbots.

However, itâs useful outside of the professional world as well. In fact, you often use artificial intelligence in your regular life without even knowing it. Many entertainment streaming platforms such as Netflix or Spotify use artificial intelligence to provide you with specialized recommendations. If you use language learning applications like Duolingo, youâre benefiting from artificial intelligence-powered lessons. Additionally, many choose to use open-source artificial intelligence to plan their weekly meals, travel itineraries, and to-do lists.

Artificial intelligence is a revolutionary tool that has optimized workplace practices as well as made improvements to daily life. It continues to expand its capabilities and find space in numerous fields and situations, so there are no signs of this field slowing down in the foreseeable future. As society progresses, all the ways that artificial intelligence can be used will become more apparent. With the right skills acquired through proper training, you will also be able to leverage your knowledge of artificial intelligence in whatever space you deem essential.

## **Why Choose a Skills Bootcamp?**

If you are a veteran and wonder what learning format to enroll in for [artificial intelligence training](/topics/ai-classes), you should consider a skills bootcamp. This option offers a focused, immersive approach to artificial intelligence training and gives you hands-on experience with the content. A bootcamp typically offers an intensive, practical experience, allowing students to leave feeling confident in their ability to apply their skills in the workforce. Unlike a traditional four-year university program, a bootcamp is relatively short and cost-effective as well.

Certificate courses are also a career-driven option that values practical application as opposed to theoretical information. One of the main advantages of choosing a certificate or bootcamp is that they operate on an accelerated timeline. They typically span a few weeks to a few months, depending on whether you choose a full-time or part-time format. In contrast, a university program will take anywhere from two years for an Associateâs degree and six years for a Masterâs degree. Plus, university programs are incredibly expensive, typically costing tens of thousands to hundreds of thousands of dollars.

Of course, bootcamps and certificates may have their downsides compared to a traditional university education. They donât go as in-depth, but depending on your reason for learning artificial intelligence, you may not need to learn the history and theory behind the subject. For a veteran who is eager to start a new career, the bootcamp or certificate will be the best option. Although they would be welcomed at a university, veterans may feel more at home in a bootcamp or certificate class. The personalized curriculum and time-tested approach to a bootcamp ensures that students still learn all the necessary skills to excel in the field of their choosing.

Overall, if you are a veteran that prioritizes entering the workforce as quickly and effectively as possible, the bootcamps and certificates will be the best option. They offer a fast-paced yet thorough approach to learning new skills and will give you hands-on experience with the tools and material. While a four-year degree is advantageous, it is not always practical. A bootcamp or certificate can be a more reasonable, comprehensive approach to learning a new skill like artificial intelligence and starting your new life back at home.

## **Learn Artificial Intelligence at Noble Desktop**

Noble Desktop offers a range of classes related to artificial intelligence. Veterans who want to learn the ins and outs of working with and training machine learning algorithms, LLMs, and other AI applications may want to consider enrolling in the VA-approved [Data Science & AI Certificate](/certificates/data-science) program. This course covers how to use Python and its associated libraries to build and train machine learning algorithms and implement those AI tools into daily workflows. This course is a great way for veterans to learn important and transferable coding skills and how to utilize AI in their regular day-to-day business. In addition, anyone enrolled in this certificate program can take the [Python for AI: Create AI Apps with Flask & OpenAI](/classes/ai-python) course for no additional cost. In this class, youâll learn how to use tools like OpenAI to create your own LLMs and AI applications.

If you know that you need artificial intelligence skills to work in a particular field or desired job position, Noble Desktop hosts courses that align with your goals. For example, they have an [AI for Graphic Design](/classes/ai-graphic-design) course. In this course, the instructor teaches you how to leverage artificial intelligence for Adobe Creative Cloud applications like Photoshop, InDesign, and Illustrator. You will learn how to use artificial intelligence to improve workflow, improve designs, and create images with Adobeâs generative artificial intelligence tool. The instructor will guide you through different tasks, like using the Lens Blur function, creating vector graphics, and generating recolor with artificial intelligence.

Perhaps you want to start using artificial intelligence for certain tasks such as improving workflow and optimizing business practices, Noble Desktop hosts an [AI for Workplace Productivity](/classes/ai-workplace-productivity) course. This course enables you to use artificial intelligence to manage different projects, automate tasks, communicate with other team members, and other basic or complex tasks. The instructor will guide you through hands-on projects after exploring case studies and explaining how to use artificial intelligence in everyday workplace situations.

--- SOURCE 4: VetsinTech: VETS in AI ---
URL: https://events.vetsintech.co/vetsinai/

SUMMARY:
Vets in AI is a new program from VetsinTech that creates pathways for veterans and mililtary spouses into the rapidly evolving AI sector.

FULL CONTENT:
![VetsinTech](images/vetsintech-logo-md.png)
![](images/head_mob.jpg)

## Vets in AI: Empowering Veterans in the Age of Artificial Intelligence

**Vets in AI** is a new program from **VetsinTech** that creates pathways for veterans and mililtary spouses into the rapidly evolving AI sector, offering opportunities in education, employment, and entrepreneurship.

**Integrating veterans and military spouses into the AI ecosystem is a matter of national security**. Veterans possess unique skills and attributes the AI sector needs. Veterans bring a deep understanding of security protocols and trustworthiness, problem-solving abilities, adaptability and teamwork makes them highly valuable to the AI industry This initiative bridges the technological hub of The Bay Area with the policy-making centers in Washington DC, fostering a unique collaboration between tech innovators, military, and policy.

Join us at the historic launch event on **2/29 at The Historic Klamath**, where distinguished leaders in AI, corporate security, and military defense will converge to mark the commencement of this transformative journey

## Our Mission

Recognizing the unparalleled value of veterans in this cutting-edge sector, Vets in AI aims to harness their distinctive skills and perspectives, offering comprehensive opportunities in education, employment, and entrepreneurship. This program not only promises to elevate the role of veterans in the AI industry but also underscores their critical contribution to national security. By connecting the technological prowess of the Bay Area with the policy-making circles of Washington DC, Vets in AI fosters an unprecedented synergy between tech leaders, military veterans, and policymakers.

![](images/ai/photo_1b.png?v=2)

## Vets in AI: Launch Event Featured Speakers

![](https://eventblaze.s3.amazonaws.com/accounts/vetsintech/thumbs/webster4.jpg)
![](https://vetsintech.s3.amazonaws.com/vetsinai/france_hoang.jpg)
![](https://vetsintech.s3.amazonaws.com/vetsinai/chris_seferlis.jpg)
![](https://vetsintech.s3.amazonaws.com/vetsinai/sam_kjellesvig.jpg)
![](https://vetsintech.s3.amazonaws.com/vetsinai/scott_wiener.jpg)
![](https://meta_sidebar.s3.amazonaws.com/host/db0935781709f67f8f600527946eb64d.png)
![](images/ai/photo_3b.png)

## Our Network

At VetsinTech, we are committed to building a robust network for veterans and military spouses transitioning into the AI sector, connecting them with an array of VCs, industry partners, and essential resources. This network is designed to provide unparalleled support and opportunities, enabling veterans to leverage cutting-edge AI technologies and integrate successfully into this dynamic field. Our partnerships and resources are tailored to nurture their skills, fostering growth and innovation in the AI landscape, thereby contributing to their success in today’s tech-driven economy.

## Join the VIT Vets in AI Coalition

![](images/sun/rocket_blue.png)

## Startups

For veterans and military spouses early-stage startups and entrepreneurs building and hiring in the AI space.

![](images/sun/money_blue.png)

## Investors

For VCs and angel investor interested in supporting and investing in AI startups founded by veteran and military spouse entrepreneurs.

![](images/sun/shake_blue.png)

## Partners

For service and technology providers interested in training veterans and military spouses on their platforms or assisting in their development on their AI stacks.

#### Benefits

#### Benefits

#### Benefits

[Apply to Join](/vetsinai/startup-application)

[Apply to Join](/vetsinai/investor-application)

[Apply to Join](/vetsinai/partner-application)

## "Artificial intelligence (AI) holds extraordinary potential for both promise and peril. Responsible AI use has the potential to help solve urgent challenges while making our world more prosperous, productive, innovative, and secure...This endeavor demands a society-wide effort that includes government, the private sector, academia, and civil society."

## Vets in AI: Our Programs

At VetsinTech, we understand that integrating into the AI sector involves more than just a basic understanding of the field. Our Vets in AI program is dedicated to equipping veterans and their spouses with the knowledge, skills, and resources needed to thrive in the artificial intelligence industry. Our programs are designed to cater to various stages of learning and development, ensuring a comprehensive understanding and application of AI technologies.

## AI Curriculum: Training

Our extensive AI Curriculum is a key component of our Vets in AI initiative. Our programs offers structured and in-depth training in key areas of AI, including machine learning, data analytics, and AI ethics. Developed in collaboration with partners from leading tech companies, this curriculum is designed to provide veterans with a solid foundation and advanced knowledge in AI.

![](images/ai/small_4b.png)
![](images/ai/small_3.png)

## AI Skills Bootcamp

An intensive, one-day program designed to immerse participants in AI basics and applications. This bootcamp covers fundamental concepts, hands-on activities with AI tools, and an overview of the AI industry, providing veterans a rapid yet thorough introduction to the field.

## AI for National Security Workshop

A specialized workshop that focuses on the application of AI in areas of national security and policy, leveraging the unique skills and experiences of veterans, military spouses and advisors, many of whom have strong govermment backgrounds. This program covers the use of AI in cybersecurity, intelligence, policy and defense strategies.

![](images/ai/small_1.png)
![](images/ai/small_5.png)

## Mentorship in AI

Ongoing mentorship opportunities with AI professionals, offering veterans guidance on career paths, project development, and staying current with AI advancements.

## Vetsintech Pitch Contest

We invite veteran and military spouse startup founders building companies the AI space to particpate in our annual VIT Pitch contest sponsored by JPMorgan Chase.

![](images/ai/small_2b.png)

 ) or **https://** means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites.

# Engage

![](https://department.va.gov/ai/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/2024/02/about-bg.png?w=1016)

There are several ways to get involved with the AI work happening at VA!

We are building communities for AI professionals, scientists, clinicians, and others interested in AI to learn about and share about artificial intelligence. From individuals just learning about AI all the way to AI experts, we encourage you to join us in building the future of AI at VA and beyond.​

Subscribe to the AI@VA Community newsletter for updates about events, competitions, news and opportunities to engage!

## AI Tech Sprints

AI Tech Sprints are three-month competitive engagements that foster collaboration between industry, academia, nonprofit organizations, and the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). Teams compete to create AI-enabled tools that leverage federal data to address specific Veteran health care issues. AI Tech Sprints accelerate VA innovations and expand VA’s ability to work with many different partners within academia and industry.

![](https://department.va.gov/ai/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/2024/02/engage-tech-sprint.png?w=298)

152 teams competed in the most recent tech sprints, making them some of the most comprehensive to date. Participants were allowed to apply for one or both tracks:

Both tracks aim to create AI solutions that reduce provider burnout by automating administrative tasks. Winners were selected on May 21, 2024. NAII is beginning to pilot these solutions at VA facilities across the nation. 

![United States Under Secretary of Veterans Affairs for Health Dr. Shareef Elnahal announces AI tech sprint award winners.](https://department.va.gov/ai/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/2024/09/Capture_479aac.png?w=839)

Former United States Under Secretary of Veterans Affairs for Health Dr. Shareef Elnahal announces AI tech sprint award winners.

NAII makes federal data available and incentivizes collaboration through the Government Innovation Framework, Challenge.Gov. All participants iteratively designed an intervention using federal and private data. Teams received access to:

![Graphic showing AI Tech Sprints Four Principles: Access to Data Partnership Collaboration Collective Good](https://department.va.gov/ai/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/2024/09/Capture_3d484f.png?w=891)

## AI Summit Series

NAII launched the AI Summit Series in 2022 as a forum for AI professionals and stakeholders to address AI’s role in improving health outcomes and health care services for Veterans.

More than 500 attendees gathered for the inaugural Brain Health and Rehabilitation through Artificial Intelligence Network (BRAIN) Summit, which highlighted how AI is impacting three areas of critical importance to Veteran health: brain cancer, traumatic brain injury (TBI) and rehabilitation, and mental health.

![](https://department.va.gov/ai/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/2024/09/Capture_44a7bd.png)
![](https://department.va.gov/ai/wp-content/uploads/sites/26/2024/02/engage-summit-series-e1707987144166.jpg?w=1482)

Nearly twice as many participants gathered in 2023 for the International Summit for AI in Health Care. Leaders in AI and health care from international governments, academia, and industry from around the world addressed how trustworthy AI – AI that is ethical, secure, and mitigates bias – can innovate and solve prevalent Veteran health issues and overcome pressing healthcare delivery challenges.

In 2024, this series has evolved into field summits at individual VA facilities. Facilities in the following cities have hosted AI summits this year, with more to come:

| Location; | Date | Topic |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Tampa Bay | Mar 7 | Al in pathology, radiology, surgery, and dermatology, trustworthy Al |
| Kansas City | Jun 25 | Cloud computing and implications, generative Al & ambient dictation, Al workforce models |
| South Central Region (Online) | Jul 24 | Al in healthcare, data and large language models, Al in patient education, CNN model for predicting pneumonia, National Al Institute trustworthy Al framework, AI model analysis |
| South West (Online) | Aug 22 | Leveraging artificial intelligence and machine learning to improve the health of Veterans and support our workforce |
| Sierra Pacific  Region (Online) | Nov 7-8 | Digital Health and state of Al in VHA, leveraging Al for the radiology workforce, trustworthy Al, ambient dictation, hands-on Al experience |
| Minneapolis | Oct | TBD |

![](/wp-content/plugins/va-corsair-basic/_media/img/seals/VA-seal.png)
